task_completion.description: Open and read the orbit requirements text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of ~/Desktop/skysurvey_orbit_requirements.txt.

task_completion.description: Configure the GMAT force model to include Earth, Moon (Luna), and Sun gravity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI showing a Force Model configuration window with Earth, Luna, and Sun selected in the Point Masses or Gravity Field sections.

task_completion.description: Set the initial spacecraft orbit parameters for a 2:1 lunar resonance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI showing the Spacecraft properties window with Keplerian elements configured (e.g., SMA between 238,000-248,000 km, Eccentricity 0.3-0.75, or Orbital Period ~13.66 days).

task_completion.description: Set the propagator to run for at least 120 days.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI showing the Propagate command configuration with the stopping condition set to ElapsedDays >= 120 (or at least 90).

task_completion.description: Save the GMAT mission script to the specified output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager, terminal, or GMAT save dialog showing 'lunar_resonant_orbit.script' located in the ~/GMAT_output/ directory.

task_completion.description: Create and save the resonance stability report with the required format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'resonance_stability_report.txt' in ~/GMAT_output/ containing the required keys (initial_SMA_km, initial_period_days, final_SMA_km, final_period_days, perigee_altitude_km, eccentricity, moon_gravity_included, stability_assessment) filled with actual values.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually ran the GMAT simulation to verify dynamic stability.
integrity.visual_evidence: GMAT application window shows an active or completed run, such as a 3D orbit plot (DefaultOrbitView) or messages in the message window indicating successful propagation.

integrity.description: Verify that the stability report contains actual numerical results rather than copied template placeholders.
integrity.visual_evidence: The resonance_stability_report.txt file shows real numbers for SMA, period, altitude, and eccentricity, and explicit 'YES'/'NO' or 'STABLE'/'UNSTABLE' text, rather than '<value>' placeholders.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just write a text file from memory without using GMAT.
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of GMAT GUI interaction or terminal execution of the GMAT script prior to the creation of the stability report.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target orbital period is 13.66 days (±5%). Semi-Major Axis (SMA) must be between 238,000 and 248,000 km. Eccentricity must be between 0.3 and 0.75 to maintain the required perigee altitude. The verifier will accept a minimum propagation of 90 days, though the prompt asks for 120 days. The script must be exactly at ~/GMAT_output/lunar_resonant_orbit.script and the report at ~/GMAT_output/resonance_stability_report.txt.
